title: Doorway Risk Audit — All 7 URLs

/loan-scenarios/small-emergency-loan-500.reason: Unique use case (small loan economics), unique explanation (why small loans cost more), not primarily funneling to calculator. Standalone educational value.

/loan-scenarios/good-credit-loan-20000-8.reason: GSC-validated demand. Serves calculator intent distinct from blog. Rich credit comparison content. Not a funnel page — user gets full answer here.

/loan-scenarios/debt-consolidation-loan-.reason: Unique consolidation decision support. Credit card comparison creates standalone value. Not replicable by number-swap. Educational + calculator hybrid.

/loan-scenarios/auto-large-purchase-loan.reason: Secured vs unsecured content is valuable but page needs deeper auto-loan-specific context. Slightly closer to a number-swap than other pages. Can be fixed.

/loan-scenarios/fair-credit-loan-20000-2.reason: Credit education + reality check framing is unique. 'Is this loan worth it?' framing creates standalone value beyond calculation. Strongest differentiation.

/loan-payment-table.reason: Hub/aggregation page — legitimate site architecture, not a doorway. Links to scenario pages contextually. Has unique methodology + FAQ content.

/data.reason: Data hub — legitimate site section. Clearly structured with citations, methodology, CSV plans. Not a doorway — this is authority-building infrastructure.

notes: The hand-crafted approach (unique use cases per page) successfully avoided doorway risk. The original 25-page mass template approach failed because it was number-swapping. These 5 pages are genuinely differentiated.
